title: Alangium vitiense
intro: Alangium vitiense, also known as the Fijian alangium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cornaceae. The health benefits of Alangium vitiense include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Alangium vitiense is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Alangium vitiense is a species of flowering plant in the family Cornaceae. It is native to the Pacific Islands and has been classified as a part of the genus Alangium, which includes several other species of plants. The family Cornaceae, to which Alangium vitiense belongs, is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogwoods. The taxonomy of Alangium vitiense has been subject to revision, with some sources classifying it as part of the family Alangiaceae. This plant is also sometimes referred to by its synonyms, including Alangium aff. vitiense and Alangium 'vitiense'.
uses: Alangium vitiense is a plant native to the Pacific Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Alangium vitiense is used to treat fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
